expectations.evidence: Formatted '## Trade-off Matrix' table with 6 quality attributes as rows and 3 options (Kafka MSK, RabbitMQ, Kinesis) as columns, with +/-/= notation per cell.
3 options analyzed: (1) Apache Kafka via Amazon MSK, (2) RabbitMQ self-managed or Amazon MQ, (3) Amazon Kinesis Data Streams.
Explicitly names driving quality attributes: Scalability/Throughput, Simplicity/Operability, Cost. Matrix adds: Event replay/Reprocessing, Ecosystem/Extensibility, Team velocity (short-term).
Clear recommendation: 'Apache Kafka via Amazon MSK Serverless' with 4-point justification covering throughput, replay, operability, and team knowledge gap analysis.
Section 'Acknowledged downsides and why they are acceptable' lists 3 explicit negatives: slower team velocity for first month, higher cost than self-managed RabbitMQ, more complex consumer model.
Recommendation explicitly states: 'Apache Kafka via Amazon MSK Serverless -- the least worst choice for this context because:'
Analysis directly references: 4 engineers, 50k events/sec peak, startup-level budget, AWS infrastructure, team's existing RabbitMQ experience, mobile app analytics use case.
Kafka has 5 disadvantages listed. RabbitMQ has 5 disadvantages. Kinesis has 5 disadvantages. All well above the 2-minimum threshold.
